reasoning: With only 45 days remaining until the July 1, 2025 deadline, the probability of an official election announcement is very low. By Ukrainian law, elections cannot be held or announced during martial law. Martial law is extended in 90-day increments, with the latest extension likely occurring in May 2025 and lasting until August 2025. Even if a ceasefire were to occur before July, lifting martial law and determining a legally legally viable election date would take considerable time. A presidential source in April 2025 strongly denied that any instructions have been given to prepare for elections following a ceasefire, indicating no immediate roadmap is in place. Furthermore, the logistical challenges of organizing an election with millions of refugees and soldiers mean the government would need significant time to study the feasibility before officially committing to a specific date. Thus, an announcement by the end of June 2025 is highly unlikely.
batch_queries: Will Ukraine announce a presidential election before July 1, 2025?
